tests_location.details: 4 tests are inline in src/widgets/message_widget.rs (lines 756-806) inside a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block. Per AGENTS.md, all tests MUST be in the tests/ folder, not inline in src/.
tests_location.recommendation: Move these 4 tests to a new file: crates/ragent-tui/tests/test_message_widget.rs. Note that `tool_input_summary` is private, so it will need to be made pub(crate) or re-exported.

naming_convention.details: All test functions follow the test_<component>_<scenario> naming convention. No violations found.

test_attributes.details: All tests use #[test]. No async tests were found in the TUI tests, which is appropriate since the TUI App is synchronous.

import_from_public_crate.details: All external test files import via `use ragent_tui::App` and `use ragent_core::*`. Public crate API is used correctly.

anti_patterns_found.description: Tests test_scroll_up_increments_offset, test_scroll_down_decrements_offset, and test_scroll_down_does_not_go_below_zero directly manipulate the `scroll_offset` field (app.scroll_offset = app.scroll_offset.saturating_add(3)) rather than calling the actual scroll action method. These tests verify arithmetic properties of saturating_add/sub, not actual application scroll behaviour. There is no actual key press or action dispatched.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Trigger scrolling via the proper app method (e.g., handle_key_event with PageUp/PageDown/arrow keys) and verify the resulting state, or at minimum call the same method the UI uses to scroll.

anti_patterns_found.description: test_log_scroll_up_increments and test_log_scroll_down_does_not_go_below_zero also directly modify log_scroll_offset instead of triggering a log scroll action.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Same as TQ-001 - use actual action dispatch.

anti_patterns_found.description: test_slash_model_no_provider_shows_warning has a conditional guard `if app.configured_provider.is_none()`. This means the test body may silently not execute if any provider env var is set in the test environment, making the test effectively a no-op in CI with provider credentials set.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Force the provider to None before calling the command to make the test deterministic: `app.configured_provider = None; app.execute_slash_command("/model");`

anti_patterns_found.description: No test covers the edge case where cycleable_agents has only one agent. The test test_agent_switch_cycles_through_agents asserts count > 1 but does not test the single-agent scenario.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Add a test that verifies graceful behaviour (no panic, no index out-of-bounds) when only one agent is cycleable.

anti_patterns_found.description: test_load_session_updates_cwd uses a hardcoded /tmp/test-project path. This session directory is never actually created, so if the implementation validates directory existence, the test would fail. The test does pass, implying directories are not validated, but this is an implicit assumption.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Either create the directory in the test with tempdir, or add a comment explaining the path is intentionally synthetic.

anti_patterns_found.description: Every test file contains an identical or near-identical `make_app()` helper function that constructs an App with in-memory storage. This creates 6 copies of essentially the same boilerplate code (~20-40 lines each). If the App constructor changes, all 6 files must be updated.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Create a shared test helper crate or a common test utility module (e.g., tests/helpers/mod.rs or a test-utils internal module) that provides a single `make_app()` factory function.

anti_patterns_found.description: The slash command tests thoroughly cover happy paths but have limited coverage for unknown/malformed commands (only one test: test_slash_unknown_command_shows_error). Edge cases like commands with extra whitespace, commands with trailing slashes, or commands with numeric arguments in wrong positions are untested.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Add tests for: /clear with extra arguments, whitespace-only commands, unicode in command names.

anti_patterns_found.description: 4 inline tests exist in the production source file inside a #[cfg(test)] block. While the tests themselves are quality tests with good assertions, their location violates the AGENTS.md requirement that all tests must be in the tests/ folder.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Migrate to tests/test_message_widget.rs. The tested function tool_input_summary is currently private (fn), so it needs to become pub(crate) or the tests need to be written against the public API.

anti_patterns_found.description: A static CWD_LOCK: Mutex<()> is defined in test_teams_tui.rs but never actually used (no test acquires the lock). This suggests the lock was intended to protect std::env::set_current_dir calls in tests, but those calls may still race if the mutex is not acquired.
anti_patterns_found.recommendation: Either remove the unused CWD_LOCK or consistently use it across any test that changes the working directory.

positive_findings: All 182 tests pass with no failures
Test files have comprehensive module-level docstrings explaining the scope and purpose
Tests are well-organised into thematic files by feature area
Agent switching tests cover cycling, events, state updates, index tracking, and exclusion of hidden agents
Scrollbar drag tests are thorough: start/stop, clamping, both panes, middle position
Session resume tests cover all side effects: messages, session_id, screen mode, status, log entries, cwd, agent preservation, error path (nonexistent ID), and empty history
Slash command tests are the most comprehensive, covering 56 test cases across all commands
Teams TUI tests (49 cases) cover slash commands, event-driven state updates, and rendering via Terminal::new(TestBackend)
Text selection tests (32 cases) cover click, drag, context menu, copy extraction with unicode, and cross-pane behaviour

warnings.message: variable does not need to be mutable: `let mut spans = vec![...]` - remove `mut`

clippy_findings_tui_only.note: Clippy was run with --no-deps to isolate warnings to ragent-tui only. The full run without --no-deps produces hundreds more warnings from ragent-core which are out of scope for this task.

top_categories.description: Variables can be used directly in format! strings (e.g., format!("{}", x) should be format!("{x}"))
format!(..) appended to an existing String - use write!() instead to avoid allocation
Redundant closures that can be replaced with direct method references
Nested if-let/if blocks that can be collapsed into a single statement
Casts (usize to u16) that may truncate - particularly in layout code that converts dimensions
unwrap() calls on Option/Result - one is a code smell (app.rs:2531 - panicking unwrap inside execute_slash_command)
u64 to usize casts that may truncate on 32-bit targets
Redundant .clone() calls where the value is immediately moved or dropped

notable_warnings.description: Panicking unwrap inside execute_slash_command. If this path is reached without a session_id, the app will crash. The code has a comment but no guard — it relies on a caller invariant that is not enforced at the type level. There is also a missing # Panics section in the doc comment.
notable_warnings.recommendation: Replace with proper error handling or an early-return guard: `let Some(sid) = &self.session_id else { return; };`

notable_warnings.description: execute_slash_command is 1619 lines long (limit is 100). This is a massive function that handles all slash commands in a single match arm sequence. This violates the cognitive complexity guidelines and makes the code difficult to maintain and test.
notable_warnings.recommendation: Extract each command handler into its own private method: fn cmd_clear(&mut self), fn cmd_help(&mut self), etc.

notable_warnings.description: Both branches of an if/else block return McpStatus::Disabled regardless of the mcp_cfg.disabled condition. This appears to be a logic bug where the 'enabled' status path was not implemented.
notable_warnings.recommendation: Review the intent: if mcp_cfg.disabled is false, should the status be McpStatus::Connected or McpStatus::Disconnected rather than Disabled?

notable_warnings.description: In percent_decode_path, a Peekable iterator is created but .peek() is never called. The peekable() call has no effect.
notable_warnings.recommendation: Remove .peekable() since peek() is unused: `let mut chars = s.chars();`

test_coverage_gaps: No tests for the tracing_layer module (crates/ragent-tui/src/tracing_layer.rs)
No tests for the logo module (crates/ragent-tui/src/logo.rs)
No tests for the tips module (crates/ragent-tui/src/tips.rs) - though doctest exists
No tests for the layout module (crates/ragent-tui/src/layout.rs - 93KB!) render functions beyond integration in teams tests
No tests for image inline display (ImageData handling in app/state.rs)
No tests for provider health check timing/retry logic
No tests for input history persistence to disk (load_history/save_history paths)
No tests for MCP server display when connected vs disconnected

recommendations_priority.action: Fix the potential logic bug at app.rs:2456 where McpStatus is always Disabled regardless of mcp_cfg.disabled flag
Replace the panicking unwrap() at app.rs:2531 with proper error handling or a guard
Move the 4 inline tests from src/widgets/message_widget.rs to tests/test_message_widget.rs to comply with AGENTS.md
Refactor test_scroll_up/down tests (TQ-001, TQ-002) to use actual scroll action dispatch rather than direct field manipulation
Extract a shared make_app() helper to eliminate duplication across 6 test files
Fix or remove the unused CWD_LOCK in test_teams_tui.rs
Make test_slash_model_no_provider_shows_warning deterministic by forcing configured_provider = None
Apply clippy auto-fixes (especially uninlined_format_args, redundant_closure, collapsible_if) to reduce warning count
Fix the unused mut warning in layout_active_agents.rs:97 (1 cargo check warning)
